summary: In this excerpt from 'Also sprach Zarathustra', Zarathustra confronts the dwarf, known as the 'Spirit of Gravity', at a gateway named 'Moment' (Augenblick). He introduces the concept of two infinite, contradictory paths of time (past and future) meeting in the present, and rebukes the dwarf for glibly reducing the profound complexity of time and eternity to a mere circle.

principles.statement: The present moment is the collision of two infinite, contradictory eternities.
principles.rationale: The past stretches back infinitely and the future stretches forward infinitely; they contradict each other but meet exactly at the gateway of the present moment.
principles.source_quote: Sie widersprechen sich, diese Wege; sie stossen sich gerade vor den Kopf: — und hier, an diesem Thorwege, ist es, wo sie zusammen kommen. Der Name des Thorwegs steht oben geschrieben: „Augenblick“.

mental_models.name: The Gateway of the Moment (Der Thorweg „Augenblick“)
mental_models.description: A spatial metaphor for time, where the present is a gateway at which the infinite past (a lane stretching back) and the infinite future (a lane stretching forward) collide.
mental_models.example: Zarathustra points out the gateway to the dwarf to illustrate how the two eternities meet and contradict each other right at the present moment.

mental_models.name: The Spirit of Gravity (Der Geist der Schwere)
mental_models.description: Personified as a dwarf sitting on Zarathustra's shoulder, representing the cynical, heavy, and oversimplifying forces that weigh down the human spirit and prevent it from bearing profound, 'abysmal' thoughts.
mental_models.example: The dwarf jumps off Zarathustra's shoulder and cynically declares that all truth is crooked and time is a circle, prompting Zarathustra's anger.

heuristics: Do not make profound truths too easy for yourself.

signature_quotes.text: Alles Gerade lügt, murmelte verächtlich der Zwerg. Alle Wahrheit ist krumm, die Zeit selber ist ein Kreis.
signature_quotes.context: The dwarf's cynical summary of time, which Zarathustra subsequently rebukes for being too simplistic.

signature_quotes.text: Du Geist der Schwere! sprach ich zürnend, mache dir es nicht zu leicht!
signature_quotes.context: Zarathustra rebuking the dwarf for treating the profound concept of eternal time as a simple platitude.

signature_quotes.text: Sie widersprechen sich, diese Wege; sie stossen sich gerade vor den Kopf: — und hier, an diesem Thorwege, ist es, wo sie zusammen kommen. Der Name des Thorwegs steht oben geschrieben: „Augenblick“.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the intersection of the past and future.

anti_patterns.description: Reducing profound, 'abysmal' concepts to simplistic, cynical platitudes.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to carry the true weight of the thought, making it 'too easy' and ignoring the inherent contradictions and depth of existence.
